turns.content:
Overview
This handoff continues the session-optimization reliability track from an evidence-audit state: implementation artifacts and ticket closures are already present, and the remaining work is review-grade validation plus final evidence packaging so the next session can confidently confirm and close out quality gates without rediscovery.

Start Point
- Working directory: repository root for context-engine.
- First orientation file: [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md).
- Board snapshot shows no active entries and no stale ownership from this pass (active_count=0, stale_count=0, conflict_count=0).

Epic Ticket / Long-Horizon Goal
- Epic tracker ticket (completed, still the organizing anchor): [./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml#L1).
- Long-horizon goal: ensure the session-api/tool-calling reliability improvements are re-validated and handed off with accurate, current evidence narrative for future audits.

Findings
1. Tracker and child bug tickets are in done state and consistent with tracker dependencies.
- [./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml#L4)
- [./.ticket/tickets/b6cdc89d-30fc-4303-aaba-f959abfeda4b/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/b6cdc89d-30fc-4303-aaba-f959abfeda4b/ticket.toml#L4)
- [./.ticket/tickets/7769da57-a8f6-4e72-a860-c8263d5a360e/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/7769da57-a8f6-4e72-a860-c8263d5a360e/ticket.toml#L4)
- [./.ticket/tickets/c851f3af-433a-496e-a586-28631de142ce/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/c851f3af-433a-496e-a586-28631de142ce/ticket.toml#L4)

2. Session-api normalization logic is present for tool-plan retagging, tool result synthesis, and ambiguous sync-terminal detection.
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs#L506)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs#L575)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs#L778)

3. Regression coverage exists for synthesized tool.execution_result and assistant.tool_plan behavior.
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs#L134)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs#L254)
- [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs#L285)

4. Ticket-api bug schema unblocking is implemented as built-in model registration.
- [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs#L14)
- [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs#L217)
- [memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/schema_registry.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/schema_registry.rs#L29)

Decisions
1. Treat current work as review-and-validation completion, not feature implementation.
2. Use persisted session artifacts as evidence pointers, but keep the handoff centered on durable repo artifacts.
3. Keep ticket references strict with full UUIDs and canonical ticket folder targets.

Blockers / Risks
1. No active execution blocker in code or ticket state.
2. Residual risk is narrative drift: handoff text can become stale if it says pending when artifacts show done.
3. Spec linkage from earlier notes should be re-confirmed in the current store snapshot before any final sign-off statement that claims spec traceability coverage.

Key Entities (with why they matter)
- Tracker ticket: [./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594/ticket.toml) (governing parent and dependency closure point).
- Child bug ticket (sync-terminal ambiguity): [./.ticket/tickets/b6cdc89d-30fc-4303-aaba-f959abfeda4b/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/b6cdc89d-30fc-4303-aaba-f959abfeda4b/ticket.toml) (source of lifecycle-state reliability fix).
- Child bug ticket (missing structured tool result payloads): [./.ticket/tickets/7769da57-a8f6-4e72-a860-c8263d5a360e/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/7769da57-a8f6-4e72-a860-c8263d5a360e/ticket.toml) (source of result-event synthesis behavior).
- Child bug ticket (empty assistant.message noise): [./.ticket/tickets/c851f3af-433a-496e-a586-28631de142ce/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/c851f3af-433a-496e-a586-28631de142ce/ticket.toml) (source of tool-plan retag/noise-reduction behavior).
- Persisted evidence session (historical root cause + transformed event stream): [.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json](.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json#L9399) and [.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json](.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json#L11375) and [.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json](.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json#L9829) (tool.execution_result, assistant.tool_plan, and ambiguity metadata evidence).
- Current session persisted history location: [.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json](.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json) and [.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/transcript.json](.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/transcript.json) (handoff continuity and stop-hook evidence).

Next Actions (execute in order)
1. Re-run targeted implementation anchor check.
- Action: confirm the three hook functions and corresponding test anchors still resolve exactly at current lines.
- First concrete command: rtk rg -n "retag_tool_only_assistant_message|build_tool_execution_result_event|is_sync_terminal_completion_ambiguous|assistant\\.tool_plan|tool\\.execution_result" memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook/tests.rs -S
- First validation check: output must include hook.rs anchors near 506, 575, 778 and hook/tests.rs anchors near 134, 254, 285.

2. Re-run focused Rust tests for changed behavior surfaces.
- Action: run focused tests in session-api and ticket-api modules that cover event normalization and bug schema registration.
- Validation expectation: all relevant tests pass with no new failures in touched logic areas.

3. Re-verify persisted evidence markers in session history.
- Action: scan [.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json](.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json) for tool.execution_result, assistant.tool_plan, and sync-terminal-state-ambiguous lifecycle metadata.
- Validation expectation: markers remain present and internally consistent with the behavior described in tickets.

4. Reconcile and refresh the final handoff/review narrative.
- Action: produce a final status note that explicitly says implementation is complete and what was re-validated in this follow-up pass.
- Validation expectation: no stale “pending/not-started” wording for already completed tickets.

Completion Status For Follow-Up Handoffs
- Action 1: not-started. Note: anchors already known from this session; rerun to ensure no drift.
- Action 2: not-started. Note: review-grade revalidation still pending in this continuation.
- Action 3: not-started. Note: evidence anchors collected, but follow-up pass must re-run command checks.
- Action 4: in-progress. Note: this handoff is the refresh scaffold; final reviewer-facing closeout still needed after Actions 1-3.

Definition Of Done
1. Ordered Actions 1-4 are completed, or explicitly marked blocked with command-level evidence.
2. Focused validation checks pass for session-api and ticket-api changes relevant to this track.
3. Final handoff/review narrative matches current ticket states and code/test evidence with no stale status text.
4. Persisted session evidence links remain auditable and consistent with claims.

Board And Persisted-Session Notes
- Board is currently clear; next agent should check in only if starting new implementation edits.
- Persisted session artifacts under [.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6](.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json) and [.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1](.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json) are diagnostic evidence; prioritize durable code/ticket findings in any final summary.

Ticket Legend
- EPIC = 1400919a-84b9-49ff-8e8a-92a7d9068594, [session-optimization] Resolve tool-calling reliability and session evidence gaps.
- BUG_SYNC = b6cdc89d-30fc-4303-aaba-f959abfeda4b, [session-optimization] run_in_terminal sync enters ambiguous background state.
- BUG_RESULT = 7769da57-a8f6-4e72-a860-c8263d5a360e, [session-optimization] session events miss structured tool result payloads.
- BUG_NOISE = c851f3af-433a-496e-a586-28631de142ce, [session-optimization] empty assistant.message events create session-noise.

Execution Instruction
Execute the Next Actions strictly in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked with concrete evidence.

explain the most important session context bloats in our tool calling setup. which calls eat up most of the tokens and which of them can be reduced the most while keeping the signal?

I will quantify this from your persisted session events so the answer is evidence-based, starting with payload-size breakdowns by tool and by event shape to identify the biggest token drivers and the highest-value reductions.

arguments.explanation: Lists recent session IDs so we can analyze current tool-calling token bloat using the most relevant sessions.
arguments.goal: Identify target sessions for payload-size analysis

turns.content: I’ve identified the heavy hitters for one large session; next I’ll compare against the earlier evidence-heavy session and then rank which high-cost calls are easiest to compress without losing debugging signal.

arguments.command:
rtk jq -r '
  def blen(x): ((x // null) | tostring | length);
  .events
  | map(select(.event_type=="tool.execution_result"))
  | group_by(.tool_name)
  | map({tool: (.[0].tool_name // "unknown"), count: length, arg_bytes: (map(blen(.tool_arguments_json))|add), result_bytes: (map(blen(.data_json))|add), total_bytes: (map(blen(.tool_arguments_json)+blen(.data_json))|add)})
  | sort_by(.total_bytes) | reverse
  | .[:12]
  | ("tool\tcount\targ_bytes\tresult_bytes\ttotal_bytes"), (.[] | [.tool,.count,.arg_bytes,.result_bytes,.total_bytes] | @tsv)
' .session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json
arguments.explanation: Computes per-tool payload-size totals for tool.execution_result events in the prior reliability session.

turns.content: I now have the aggregate bloat profile; I’m pulling the largest individual events next so we can pinpoint exactly which call patterns are worth changing versus keeping as-is.

arguments.command:
rtk jq -r '
  def blen(x): ((x // null) | tostring | length);
  .events
  | to_entries
  | map(select(.value.event_type=="tool.execution_result"))
  | map({line:(.key+1), tool:(.value.tool_name // "unknown"), bytes:(blen(.value.tool_arguments_json)+blen(.value.data_json)), arg_bytes:blen(.value.tool_arguments_json), data_bytes:blen(.value.data_json), command: (.value.tool_arguments_json.command // .value.data_json.arguments.command // "")})
  | sort_by(.bytes) | reverse | .[:12]
  | ("line\ttool\tbytes\targ_bytes\tdata_bytes\tcommand"), (.[] | [.line,.tool,.bytes,.arg_bytes,.data_bytes,.command] | @tsv)
' .session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json
arguments.explanation: Finds largest individual tool execution result events in session 281f with command context.
arguments.goal: Identify specific high-bloat tool call patterns

turns.content: I have enough raw data; I’m running one compact ranking pass that converts payload bytes to relative share so I can answer which call classes are most expensive and most reducible without losing signal.

arguments.command:
rtk jq -r '
  def blen(x): ((x // null) | tostring | length);
  .events
  | map(select(.event_type=="tool.execution_result"))
  | group_by(.tool_name)
  | map({tool:(.[0].tool_name // "unknown"), bytes:(map(blen(.tool_arguments_json)+blen(.data_json))|add)})
  | (map(.bytes)|add) as $tot
  | map(. + {pct: (if $tot>0 then ((.bytes*10000/$tot)|round)/100 else 0 end)})
  | sort_by(.bytes) | reverse | .[:8]
  | ("tool\tbytes\tpct"), (.[] | [.tool,.bytes,.pct] | @tsv)
' .session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json && echo '---' && rtk jq -r '
  def blen(x): ((x // null) | tostring | length);
  .events
  | map(select(.event_type=="tool.execution_result"))
  | group_by(.tool_name)
  | map({tool:(.[0].tool_name // "unknown"), bytes:(map(blen(.tool_arguments_json)+blen(.data_json))|add)})
  | (map(.bytes)|add) as $tot
  | map(. + {pct: (if $tot>0 then ((.bytes*10000/$tot)|round)/100 else 0 end)})
  | sort_by(.bytes) | reverse | .[:8]
  | ("tool\tbytes\tpct"), (.[] | [.tool,.bytes,.pct] | @tsv)
' .session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json
arguments.explanation: Ranks tool result payload share by tool across both large sessions for direct optimization prioritization.

turns.content:
Most important context bloat in your current tool-calling setup, based on two large persisted sessions:
- [./.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json](./.session/sessions/281f855b-f0ee-4195-800a-733010ed43e6/events.json)
- [./.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json](./.session/sessions/a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1/events.json)

Top token eaters by tool.execution_result payload share
1. apply_patch and run_in_terminal dominate.
2. In session 281f:
- run_in_terminal: 59212 bytes, 37.99%
- apply_patch: 50455 bytes, 32.37%
- read_file: 31345 bytes, 20.11%
3. In session a022:
- apply_patch: 56955 bytes, 47.01%
- run_in_terminal: 36527 bytes, 30.15%
- read_file: 11949 bytes, 9.86%
- grep_search: 11805 bytes, 9.74%

Most important hidden bloat pattern
1. Triple event duplication around each tool call is very expensive:
- tool.execution_start
- tool.execution_result
- tool.execution_complete
2. In session 281f this trio is roughly:
- result: 155873 bytes
- start: 136549 bytes
- complete: 61686 bytes
3. This means large argument blobs (especially command strings and patch text) are repeated multiple times.

Which calls can be reduced the most while keeping signal
1. apply_patch (highest reduction potential, low signal loss risk)
- Why bloated: full patch text appears in arguments and repeats across event phases; single events reached about 24069 bytes.
- Keep signal by storing only:
  - file list
  - hunk headers
  - line counts
  - patch hash
  - optional first N lines preview
- Fetch full patch body only on demand by hash.
- Likely reducible by 70 to 90% for patch-heavy sessions.

2. run_in_terminal (very high potential, low to medium risk)
- Why bloated: long inline shell commands, especially heredoc script creation, are embedded directly; one event reached about 14655 bytes.
- Keep signal by storing:
  - normalized command summary
  - command hash
  - short argument preview
  - structured outcome fields
- Move large scripts to file artifacts and reference path plus hash instead of inlining.
- Likely reducible by 50 to 80% on terminal-heavy sessions.

3. read_file (high cumulative cost, low risk)
- Why bloated: large read windows and repeated broad reads.
- Keep signal by defaulting to:
  - tighter ranges
  - targeted symbol windows
  - truncation summaries with byte and line counts
- Likely reducible by 30 to 60% with minimal analysis quality loss.

4. assistant.message and assistant.tool_plan verbosity (medium potential, medium risk)
- assistant.message is large in both sessions, including multi-kilobyte narrative turns.
- assistant.tool_plan sometimes carries large embedded argument blocks.
- Keep signal by emitting compact plan objects and short progress deltas, and link to artifacts for long prose.
- Likely reducible by 25 to 50% without harming operational clarity.

Practical priority order for your setup
1. De-duplicate tool arguments across start/result/complete via shared payload id.
2. Compact apply_patch storage to metadata plus hash, lazy-load full patch.
3. Normalize run_in_terminal commands and externalize long inline scripts.
4. Enforce bounded read_file defaults and escalate to larger reads only when needed.
5. Trim assistant narrative and tool plan payload size with artifact references.

If you want, I can turn this into a concrete policy checklist for your hook layer in [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/hook.rs) with exact field-level rules for what to keep, hash, truncate, and spill.

What was delivered

Overview
This checkpoint is committed and ready for continuation. The implementation track delivered session audit support in session-api, wired it into audit-cli through the unified audit run interface for latest or explicit session selection, added persisted session schema version tagging/validation, and closed the core implementation tickets. A follow-up planning ticket is now open for policy-file updates and generated guidance synchronization.

Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
None used.

Findings
- Checkpoint commit completed in memory-api: 73fcd4a feat(session-api): add session_audit and schema-versioned persistence.
- Root checkpoint commit completed: f9d9c690 chore(session-audit): checkpoint implementation and handoff planning artifacts.
- Session-api now has a dedicated session audit model/report surface in [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/audit.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/audit.rs).

- Audit-cli unified run path now supports session-focused review selectors in [memory-api/tools/cli/audit-cli/src/cli.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/audit-cli/src/cli.rs).
- Live command evidence confirms both selector paths work:
  - latest: audit --json run . --latest-session --session-store-root .session --session-workspace-slug context-engine
  - explicit: audit --json run . --session-id a0228f9f-bbac-4c82-b1e6-8a628aa91ec1 --session-store-root .session --session-workspace-slug context-engine
- Follow-up policy planning ticket created and linked: [474eb962-b68f-4651-b980-c4c9233b2710](.ticket/tickets/474eb962-b68f-4651-b980-c4c9233b2710/ticket.toml).

Decisions

- Keep implementation and validation commits separate from policy/rule-source updates.
- Track policy/instruction updates as explicit follow-up work to avoid mixing with core feature delivery.
- Preserve strict traceability through ticket/spec artifacts in the root store and submodule pointer commit.

Blockers And Risks
